headline: Rafizi and Nik Nazmi resigned. The Constitution lets both seats stay empty.
context: On 17 May 2026, ex-ministers Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced they would quit PKR and vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa to take over Parti Bersama Malaysia. Article 54(1)'s second proviso lets both seats stay empty until GE16, unless the Speaker calls a by-election.

cards.big: Two ex-ministers quit PKR and took over an existing party with no MPs. The buried line: their two seats can lawfully stay empty until GE16.
cards.sub: PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke confirmed it on 17 May: 'no necessity to have a by-election.' Article 54(1)'s second proviso permits it.

cards.big: Pandan and Setiawangsa are set to vacate on 18 May. Article 54(1) lets such seats sit empty when fewer than two years remain in Parliament's mandate.
cards.sub: GE15 was 19 November 2022. The 15th Parliament first sat 19 December 2022; its two-year carve-out window opened 19 December 2025. Both seats sit inside it.

cards.big: Resign the seat first, leave the party second. The MPs say that sequence avoids Article 49A and may sidestep PKR's RM10 million bond.
cards.sub: The Court of Appeal already cut former PKR VP Zuraida Kamaruddin's RM10M to RM100,000 in Dec 2024, ruling the sum unreasonable under the Contracts Act. She had not resigned her seat first; the seat-first route is legally untested.

cards.big: The carve-out used to be six months. A constitutional amendment expanded it to two years. Constitutional scholar Shad Saleem Faruqi called the change regrettable.
cards.sub: Pandan and Setiawangsa fall inside the two-year window. By-elections happen only if the Speaker tells the EC the governing majority is affected.

cards.big: Anti-hopping was sold as protecting the voter's mandate. Resign the seat before the party, and the sequence circumvents the law without breaking it.
Like a residents' association chairman resigning 18 months before the AGM, with by-laws blocking any election before then. The chair, by default, stays empty.
cards.sub: Pandan and Setiawangsa just emptied two MP chairs the same way. The Constitution permits the default explicitly.

cards.big: Two Klang Valley seats may sit empty until the next general election because the Constitution permits it by default. Parliament wrote that rule; only Parliament can revisit it.
